title: Oku

The '''Oku''' were a [[Color/Legends|white]]-[[Hair/Legends|furred]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of primitive [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] native to the frigid, [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]]-rich [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tokmia/Legends|Tokmia]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The [[Figg Excavations/Legends|Figg Excavations]] [[Mining/Legends|mining]] company exhausted Tokmia's mines and eventually abandoned the planet, leaving behind a [[Oku cargo cult|cargo cult]] among the natives. Remembering the handouts that Figg employees gave them during each mining operation, the Oku lit huge [[Fire/Legends|fires]] to serve as landing lights in the hope that the offworlders would one [[Standard day/Legends|day]] return.

==Biology and appearance==
The Oku were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Color/Legends|white]]-[[Hair/Legends|furred]],<ref name="Hoth and the Greater Javin" /> barely-[[Sentience/Legends|sentient]]<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Alien/Legends|aliens]].<ref name="Hoth and the Greater Javin" /> They survived among the vast, frozen flatlands of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Tokmia/Legends|Tokmia]], a tundra [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE" /> with 90 percent standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] and a Type I [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]].<ref name="Hoth and the Greater Javin" />

==Society and culture==
The Oku were primitive in relation to [[Realspace/Legends|spacefaring]] technology and governed themselves in tribal councils. A population of 400,000 Oku lived on Tokmia by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="Hoth and the Greater Javin" />

==History==
The Oku evolved on the frozen terrestrial planet Tokmia,<ref name="CSWE" /> located in the remote [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Hoth and the Greater Javin" /> Tokmia boasted plentiful [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] and [[Ore/Legends|ore]] deposits, which once attracted the interest of the [[Figg Excavations/Legends|Figg Excavations]] [[Mining/Legends|mining]] company. Figg Excavations earned a fortune from Tokmia's [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]] bounty, and Figg employees left handouts for the Oku natives with each operation. When the mines ran dry, however, Figg Excavations pulled out and abandoned the planet.<ref name="CSWE" /> The Oku developed a bizarre [[Oku cargo cult|cargo cult]] in response,<ref name="Hoth and the Greater Javin" /> burning huge [[Fire/Legends|fires]]<ref name="CSWE" /> in patterns reminiscent of landing lights and prophesying that the [[Magic/Legends|magical]] [[Starship/Legends|starships]] of their benevolent offworld visitors would [[Standard day/Legends|someday]] return.<ref name="Hoth and the Greater Javin" />

==Behind the scenes==
The [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Wizards.com|online]] article "[[Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin]]," a ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement, first mentioned the Oku among its description of the planet Tokmia.<ref name="Hoth and the Greater Javin" />

The Oku cargo cult closely resembles the real-world anthropological phenomenon of [[Wikipedia:Cargo cult|cargo cults]], particularly those that developed among some indigenous [[Wikipedia:Pacific Islands|Pacific Island]] cultures in the aftermath of [[Wikipedia:World War II|World War II]]. [[Wikipedia:Melanesians|Melanesians]], for example, benefited from the vast amounts of war matériel that both [[Wikipedia:United States|American]] and [[Wikipedia:Japan|Japanese]] forces introduced to their culture. Once the war ended, some Melanesians lit signal fires, among other acts designed to mimic soldiers, believing they could compel the return of the foreigners' material goods.<ref name="Scientific American">{{WebCite|url=https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/1959-cargo-cults-melanesia|text=50 Years Ago: Cargo Cults of Melanesia|author=Worsley, Peter M.|date=2009-04-24|work=[[Wikipedia:Scientific American|''Scientific American'']]|archivedate=20170922170015}}</ref><ref name="Astral Traveler">{{WebCite|url=http://www.astraltraveler.com/cargo-cults.html|text=.:. Cargo Cults .:. ASTRAL TRAVELER .:.|author=Seagan, Danyel|work=Astral Traveler|archivedate=20130606023744}}</ref>
